concerns.text: The 'Literature Merge Fallback' strategy listed in Complexity Tracking has no corresponding task in the plan. The spec (Edge Cases) mandates a hard halt if < 500 samples; the plan introduces an unimplemented scope expansion (merging literature data) without a task to validate sources or implement the merge logic, leaving FR-001/SC-001 unmet if APIs fail.

The plan states 'Hard halt on sample count < 500' but the Complexity Tracking section introduces a 'Literature Merge Fallback' to meet this threshold. This contradiction leaves the spec's Edge Case (hard halt) ambiguous in the plan. There is no plan element defining the logic for the fallback or the validation of literature sources, creating a gap for FR-001.

The plan lists `code/data/fetch_headata.py` for FR-001/FR-010 but does not explicitly detail the logic for recording 'API versions' in `source_metadata.yaml`. While FR-010 and Constitution Principle VI require recording both 'API versions and query parameters', the plan's description of the fetch step only mentions 'API fetch, metadata' without specifying the version recording requirement, risking non-compliance with FR-010.

The plan lists `code/data/preprocess.py` for FR-002/FR-003 but does not explicitly detail the logic for excluding Miedema-derived features from the predictor set when the target is Residual Bulk Modulus. FR-002 and FR-008 mandate this exclusion to prevent circular validation. The plan's description of the preprocess step only mentions 'ILR, normalization, Miedema exclusion' but lacks the specific conditional logic (if target == residual, exclude Miedema features) required to satisfy FR-008.

The plan lists `code/data/validate.py` for FR-009 but does not explicitly detail the behavior (log warning + proceed with caution) if the correlation between residuals and descriptors exceeds |r| > 0.1. FR-009 mandates this specific behavior. The plan's description of the validate step only mentions 'Residual correlation check' without specifying the control flow, leaving the requirement unmet.

The plan lists `code/models/evaluate.py` for FR-005/FR-006 but does not explicitly detail the implementation of 'permutation testing' for Type I error rate estimation. FR-006 mandates reporting the 'Type I error rate (estimated via permutation testing)'. The plan's description of the evaluate step only mentions 'Bootstrap, FDR, Permutation test' but does not specify the permutation testing methodology required for FR-006, risking an incorrect deliverable.

The plan lists `code/models/evaluate.py` for FR-005 but does not explicitly detail the fallback behavior for grouped bootstrap if unique groups < 10. The spec (Edge Cases) mandates a warning and fallback to standard bootstrap. The plan's description of the evaluate step only mentions 'Bootstrap' without specifying the group count threshold or the fallback logic, leaving the Edge Case requirement unmet.

The plan lists `code/reports/generate_report.py` for FR-007/US-3 but does not explicitly detail the inclusion of the mandatory associational disclaimer. FR-007 mandates the exact string 'These findings are associational and do not imply causation'. The plan's description of the report step only mentions 'Associational disclaimer, SHAP' without specifying the exact string requirement, risking non-compliance with FR-007.
